content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 23 , 1999


Jeanne Bunch
City Clerk
201 North Broadway
Escondido, CA 92025

Re: Combined Campaign Reports
Our File No. I-99-183

Dear Ms Bunch:

This is a response to the request from the San Diego County Registrar's office regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").

The San Diego County Registrar's office will be conducting a special election on behalf of the City of Escondido. This letter constitutes written advice under Section 84205.

Section 84205 provides:

"The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit
candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining
statements and reports required to be filed by this title."

A special election has been called for Proposition A to be voted upon within the City of Escondido on August 17, 1999. For primarily formed committees filing statements in connection with this election, the first pre-election campaign statement required to be filed is due July 8, 1999, and covers the period January 1, 1999 through July 3, 1999. Because the semi-annual statement filing deadline for primarily formed ballot measure committees is July 31, 1999, which falls after the deadline for the first pre-election statement, committees formed to support or oppose the measure should file reports as follows:

File No. I-99-183
Page 2


Type of Statement                    Period Covered              Filing Deadline

First Pre-Election                   1/1/99 - 7/3/99            7/8/99

Second Pre-Election                  7/4/99 - 7/31/99           8/5/99

Late Contributions &
Late Independent
Expenditures of $1,000
or More                              8/1/99 - 8/16/99           24 Hours

Semi-Annual                          8/1/99 - 12/31/99          1/31/00




If you have any questions regarding this, please feel free to call me at 916/322-5660.

                                     Sincerely,

                                     Steven G. Churchwell
                                     General Counsel

                                     [signature: Mary Ann Kvasager]

                                     By: Mary Ann Kvasager
                                         Political Reform Consultant

cc:  Elvira Vargas
     San Diego County Registrar's Office

August 17, 1999
Election Filing Schedule
Ballot Measure Committees


Filing                Type of              Period Covered 1/        Method of
Deadline              Statement            by Statement             Delivery

July                  First Pre-election   1/1/99 - 7/3/99         • Personal Delivery
8                                                                   • First Class Mail


August                Second Pre-election  7/4/99 - 7/31/99        • Personal Delivery
5                                                                   • Guaranteed Overnight
                                                                    Service


24                    Late Contributions   8/1/99 - 8/16/99        • Personal Delivery
Hours 2/              and Late Independent                          • Telegram
                      Expenditures of $1,000                        • Guaranteed Overnight
                      or more                                       Service
                                                                    • Fax


January               Semi-Annual          8/1/99 - 12/31/99       • Personal Delivery
31                                                                  • First Class Mail



1/  The period covered by any statement begins on the day after the
    closing date of the last statement filed, or January 1, if no previous
    statement has been filed.

2/  The recipient of a late "in-kind" contribution must file a Late
    Contribution Report within 48 hours from the time the in-kind
    contribution is received.

Note:

•   Semi-annual and pre-election campaign statements of 30 pages or
    less may be mailed if the original statement is sent within 24
    hours of the filing deadline. Late Contribution and Late
    Independent Expenditure Reports may be faxed but are not also
    required to be mailed.




                                          Refer to appropriate campaign disclosure manuals to
                                          determine where to file campaign statements




                                                                    Prepared by Fair Political Practices Commission
                                                                    (916) 322-5660  •  www.fppc.ca.gov
